questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right] \mathrm{Cl}_3$ has a magnetic moment of 3.83 BM . Which of the following distribution of $3 d$ electrons in chromium ion of the complex is incorrect? [Atomic number of $\mathrm{Cr}=24$ ]
questions.solution: The $\mathrm{Cr}^{3+}$ ion in the given octahedral complex has 3 electrons in its $3 d$ subshell which are accommodated in the $t_{2 g}$ level consisting of $d_{x y} d_{y z}$ and $d_{x z}$ which have lower energy than $e_g$ level consisting of $d_{x^2-y^2}$ and $d_{z^2}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider the following reactions of complex compounds $\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}$ and C
(i) $\mathrm{CoCl}_2 \mathrm{Br} \cdot 5 \mathrm{NH}_3+$ excess $\mathrm{Ag}^{+}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow 1 \mathrm{AgCl}(\mathrm{s})$
(Compound A)
(ii) $\mathrm{CoCl}_2 \mathrm{Br} \cdot 5 \mathrm{NH}_3+$ excess $\mathrm{Ag}^{+}($aq $) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{AgCl}($ s $)$
(Compound B)
(iii) $\underset{\text { (Compound } \mathrm{C} \text { ) }}{\mathrm{CoCl}_3 \mathrm{Br} \cdot 4 \mathrm{NH}_3}+$ excess $\mathrm{Ag}^{+}(\mathrm{aq}) \longrightarrow 1 \mathrm{AgCl}(\mathrm{s})$

Then according to the given information the correct statement(s) is/are
options: Molar conductivity of compounds ( A ) and (B) are almost same
Compounds (A), (B) and (C) do not exhibit geometrical isomerism
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A d-block element forms octahedral complex but its magnetic moment remains same either in strong field or In weak field ligand. Which of the following is/are correct?
options: Number of electrons in $t_{2g}$, orbitals are higher than in $e_g$, orbitais
It can have either $d^3$ or $d^8$ configuration
It can have either $d^7$ or $d^8$ configuration
questions.solution:
Irrespective of nature of ligand field, magnetic moment of octahedral complexes having metal ions in $d^1, d^2$, $d^3, d^8, d^9$ configuration, remains same.
Element may or may not form colouriess complex.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Regarding the co-ordination compound "Tetrammine dithocyanatocobalt (III) trioxalatocobaltate (III)" the comrect statements are:
questions.solution: The correct formula of the complex is $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_4(\mathrm{SCN})_2\right]_2\left[\mathrm{Co}(\mathrm{OX})_2\right]$. The anionic part of the complex exhibits optical isomerism and is a chelate complex. The cationic part of the complex exhibits geometrical isomerism.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Identify the complexes which are expected to be coloured. Give explanation.
questions.solution:
2. (A) In $\left[\mathrm{Ti}\left(\mathrm{NO}_3\right)_4\right], \mathrm{O} . \mathrm{N}$. of Ti is +4 .

Hence, in Ti (IV) $\left(1 s^2, 2 s^2, 2 p^6, 3 s^2, 3 p^6\right)$ incomplete $d$-orbital is not present. So, it is colourless.
(B) In $\left[\mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{NCCH}_3\right)\right]^{+} \mathrm{BF}_4, \mathrm{O}$. N. of Cu is +1 . Hence, in $\mathrm{Cu}(\mathrm{I})\left[1 \mathrm{~s}^2, 2 \mathrm{~s}^2, 2 \mathrm{p}^6, 3 \mathrm{~s}^2, 3 \mathrm{p}^6, 3 \mathrm{~d}^{10}\right]$ incomplete d-orbital is not present. So it is also colourless.
(C) In $\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_6\right]^{3+} 3 \mathrm{Cl}$, O.N. of Cr is +3 . Hence, in Cr (III) $\left(1 \mathrm{~s}^2, 2 \mathrm{~s}^2 2 \mathrm{p}^6, 3 \mathrm{~s}^2, 3 \mathrm{p}^6, 3 \mathrm{~d}^3\right)$ incomplete $\mathrm{d}-$ orbitals are present. So, it is coloured.
(D) In $\mathrm{K}_3\left[V F_6\right]$, O.N. of V is +3 . Hence, in V(III) $\left(1 s^2, 2 s^2 2 p^6, 3 s^2, 3 p^6, 3 d^2\right)$ incomplete d-orbital is present, so it is coloured.
Hence, (C) and (D) are the correct answers.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

Complex is stabilized by internal hydrogen bonding

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Which of the following is true regarding the following coordination compounds -
$\mathrm{CrCl}_3 \cdot 6 \mathrm{NH}_3, \mathrm{PtCl}_4 \cdot 4 \mathrm{NH}_3, \mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NO}_2\right)_3 \cdot 3 \mathrm{KNO}_2, \mathrm{PtCl}_2 \cdot 2 \mathrm{NH}_3$
options: $\mathrm{PtCl}_4 \cdot 4 \mathrm{NH}_3$ has maximum electrical conductance at infinite dilution.
$\mathrm{CrCl}_3 \cdot 6 \mathrm{NH}_3$ has the maximum spin only magnetic moment.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The compound(s) that exhibit(s) geometrical isomerism or optical isomerism or both is(are) :
questions.solution:
(A) $\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{en}) \mathrm{Br}_2\right]$ :
exists only in one form. (B) $\left[\mathrm{Pt}(\mathrm{en})_3\right] \mathrm{Cl}_4$ :
(C) $\left[\mathrm{NiCl}_2 \mathrm{Br}_2\right]^{2-}=$ tetrahedral, no, GI, no optical isomerism (D) $\left[\mathrm{Pt}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_2(\mathrm{CN})_2\right]: \mathrm{Pt}$ is in +2 oxidation state having $5 \mathrm{~d}^8$ configuration. Hence the hybridisation of complex is $\mathrm{dsp}^2$ and geometry is square planar.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: In the crystal field of the complex $\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{Cl})(\mathrm{CN})_4\left(\mathrm{O}_2\right)\right]^{4-}$ the electronic configuration of metal is found to be $\mathrm{t}_{2 \mathrm{~g}}^6, \mathrm{e}_{\mathrm{g}}^0$ then which of the following is true about this complex ion :
options: $\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{O}$ bond length will be less than found in $\mathrm{O}_2$ molecule
Its IUPAC name will be chlorotetracyanosuperoxidoferrate (II) ion
questions.solution:
The given complex is actually
hence $\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{II})$ is $\mathrm{t}_{2 \mathrm{~g}}{ }^6, \mathrm{e}_{\mathrm{g}}{ }^0$ due to effect of strong ligands but it is paramagnetic due to $\mathrm{O}_2{ }^{-1}$ ligand.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following is/are correct
options: $\left[\mathrm{Cu}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_4\right]^{2+},\left[\mathrm{Pt}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_4\right]^{2+}$ and $\left[\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CN})_4\right]^{2-}$, all have dsp ${ }^2$ hybridization of central metal
It is difficult to substitute chelating ligands compared to similar monodentate ligands
questions.solution: Both complexes have $\mathrm{Cr}^{+3}\left(\mathrm{~d}^3\right)$, and coordination number is 6 . Hence $\mathrm{d}^2 s p^3$ $\mathrm{Pd}^{+2}\left(\mathrm{~d}^8\right)$ complexes with coordination number $\rightarrow 4$ are square plannar and magnetic moment is zero.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Which of the following properties is/are same in:
(I) $\left[\mathrm{CrCl}_6\right]^{3-}$
(II) $\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{3+}$
(III) $\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_6\right]^{3+}$
(IV) $\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_6\right]^{3-}$
questions.solution: $\mathrm{Cr}^{3+}, \mathrm{d}^3$ configuration shows same number paired $\mathrm{e}^{-}$and EAN but strength of all ligands are different so does not slow same colour and splitting.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
